channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Six Held as DCI Investigates Unauthorized Mass Grave in Kericho
item.description: Investigations into a mass grave in Kericho County have intensified, with six people now held in custody as the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI), led by Mohamed Amin, seeks to uncover how ...

item.title: Pakistan Emphasises Mediating Role in Iran-US Dialogue Amid Rising Regional Tensions
item.description: Pakistan’s Ambassador to the United States, Rizwan Saeed Sheikh, has reiterated Islamabad’s dedication to facilitating dialogue between the US and Iran, even as tensions in the region remain high. In ...

item.title: Butter-coated soft serve stirs debate over taste and health at Sydney Easter Show
item.description: At the Sydney Royal Easter Show, a new butter-dipped soft serve ice-cream has captured public attention — not so much for its flavour, but for its Instagram-worthy appearance. The treat features a swi...

item.title: Horoscope Predictions for Each Zodiac Sign on March 31, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail’s daily horoscope for March 31, 2026, written by Sally Brompton, delivers insights for all twelve zodiac signs. The piece opens with advice for people whose birthday falls on this d...

item.title: Ford South Africa issues recall for Transit and Tourneo vehicles due to potential battery gas hazard
item.description: Ford South Africa has announced a recall of 582 Transit and Tourneo Custom vehicles, covering both current and previous generations sold between 2022 and 2026. The decision follows a notification from...

item.title: DPM Ishaq Dar visits China to discuss regional peace and bilateral ties
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has departed for China on a one-day official visit at the invitation of Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi. This visit comes as Pakistan intensifies ...

item.title: Rajasthan High Court criticises changes to transgender rights under the 2026 Amendment Bill
item.description: The Rajasthan High Court has raised serious constitutional concerns over the 2026 Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, observing that it turns a basic human right — the right to ...

item.title: Nollywood and Nigeria Cinema: How Local Films Fit in the Market
item.description: For Nigeria, e be like say we dey watch as foreign films chop our cinema market while local movies dey struggle. In 2025, Warner Bros. and Disney dey control over 66 percent of the Nigerian and Anglop...

item.title: Pakistan and Other Nations Protest Israeli Restrictions on Religious Worship in Jerusalem
item.description: Pakistan, together with seven other countries including Egypt, Jordan, UAE, Indonesia, Turkiye, Saudi Arabia and Qatar, has strongly condemned Israel's restrictions on the freedom of worship for Musli...

item.title: African Scholars Call for Locally-Led Conservation Models
item.description: Dar es Salaam – For long time, Africa, including Tanzania, has been told how to look after its land, forests and wildlife with rules and policies made mostly abroad. Many conservation programs promise...

item.title: Tanzania Calls for Stronger Regional Cooperation in AI to Boost Development Across EAC Sectors
item.description: During the 4th East African Community (EAC) Regional Science, Technology, and Innovation Conference held in Kigali, Rwanda, Tanzanian Minister of Foreign Affairs and East African Cooperation Mahmoud T...

item.title: Alberta government seeks constitutional amendment for greater role in judicial appointments
item.description: The Alberta government, led by Premier Danielle Smith, is urging Ottawa to amend the Canadian Constitution to give provinces more influence in appointing superior and appeal court judges. This call co...

item.title: Family mourns victim of accident involving Defence CS Duale’s convoy, laments lack of government assistance
item.description: A grieving family in Kenya is crying out for support after losing a loved one in a tragic road accident involving the convoy of Defence Cabinet Secretary Aden Duale. The incident, which occurred recen...

item.title: Winnipeg’s Dunstone team dominates Poland 9-2 at men’s world curling championship
item.description: After a challenging start at the Men’s World Curling Championship, Dunstone’s rink from Winnipeg delivered an impressive performance against Poland, securing a decisive 9-2 victory. The team displayed...

item.title: Kabelo Lekalakala’s Ostrich Farm Offers Hope for Young Farmers in North West
item.description: Kabelo Lekalakala, a 36-year-old entrepreneur from Brits, North West, has made a name for himself in the agricultural sector through his ostrich farm, Pitso Ostrich Farm. He entered the industry in 20...

item.title: SAUS urges urgent reforms after NSFAS funding goes to deceased and ineligible recipients
item.description: The South African Union of Students (SAUS) has demanded swift and transparent changes at the National Student Financial Aid Scheme (NSFAS) after the auditor-general uncovered serious mismanagement. Th...

item.title: Three-Vehicle Crash on I-215 in Riverside Kills Infant, Injures Others
item.description: A tragic three-vehicle collision occurred on Interstate 215 in Riverside on Sunday evening, resulting in the death of a 3-month-old infant, Leon Edgington Morales, and injuries to five other people. T...

item.title: Implementing Agentic AI in Canadian Businesses: Moving from Pilot to Production
item.description: Many Canadian businesses have spent recent years experimenting with generative AI, mostly through chatbots that answer questions. The next phase, agentic AI, goes beyond simple responses by planning w...

item.title: Cathy Freeman joins Brisbane 2032 Olympic advisory group to promote Indigenous role and unity
item.description: Australian Olympic legend Cathy Freeman has taken on a new role to help shape the Brisbane 2032 Olympic Games, more than two decades after her iconic 400-metre gold medal win at the Sydney 2000 Games....

item.title: Clara Immerwahr: Pioneering German Chemist and Her Struggle Against Gender and Moral Constraints
item.description: Clara Immerwahr was born in 1870 in Polkendorf, Germany, and became the first woman in Germany to earn a PhD in Chemistry in 1900, overcoming significant institutional barriers. Despite her achievemen...
